cmes.note: Faint CME first seen to the North by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-03-02T01:26Z, as well by SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 in later frames. This CME is not visible in GOES CCOR-1 due to a brief eclipse period. The source of this event is likely far sided, but field line opening can be seen along the NE limb in GOES SUVI 195 beginning at approx. 2026-03-01T23:48Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: Bright CME first seen to the North in SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-03-02T09:24Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. The source of this event is the eruption of a filament located at approx. N40E35 which was heavily deflected to the North as seen in GOES SUVI 304. This filament became unstable at approx. 2026-03-02T00:44Z and initially attempted to erupt, but most of the material fell back to the disk. This filament finally began to fully erupt at approx. 2026-03-02T08:44Z. Field line opening, minor dimming and post eruptive arcades are also visible in GOES SUVI 195 and 284 in association with this eruption.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the West by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-03-02T10:36Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This CME is not clearly visible in STEREO A COR2 as it is likely obscured by the occulting disk. The source of this event is a filament eruption located at approx. N05W65 as seen in GOES SUVI 304, which began lifting off at approx. 2026-03-02T09:52Z. Field line opening is also visible in GOES SUVI 195 and 284 in association with this event.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: Narrow CME first seen to the SW by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-03-02T13:36Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This event is not clearly seen in STEREO A COR2. There is no clear source for this CME, aside from some minor field line movement around 2026-03-02T12:30Z along the SW limb seen in GOES SUVI 195.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky using a longitude of W90 due to lack of clear source and no STEREO A COR2 imagery to triangulate.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the North by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-03-02T15:36Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. The source of this event is continued dimming and field line movement seen in GOES SUVI 195 located at approx. N50E20 following an earlier filament eruption and resulting CME: 2026-03-02T09:24Z, which this CME heavily overlaps.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: CME seen to the north in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1. Not seen in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap from 2026-03-02T16:23Z to 2026-03-03T04:09Z. The potential source eruption is seen in GOES SUVI 195 imagery around N25W20, north of AR 14383, starting at 2026-03-02T22:24Z as dimming and post-eruptive arcades.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on source location using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery in SWPC_CAT.

cmes.note: CME faintly visible to the east in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1. Not clearly visible in STEREO A COR2. The source is not found, and the CME may be farsided.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane of sky measurement using SOHO LASCO C2 running difference imagery in SWPC_CAT.

cmes.note: Partial halo CME seen to the east in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 and the west in STEREO A COR2. The CME is far-sided, and no source can be seen in available EUV imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement of the faster, fainter halo shock feature seen in STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Updated measurement of the leading edge based on the best fit between GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.
Preliminary measurement of leading edge using early frames of GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT. Based on the best fit.

cmes.note: CME visible to the NNW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 and the NNE in STEREO A COR2. The source is not very clear in available EUV imagery, but may be an eruption from the vicinity of AR 14378 (approximately N15E05). This eruption is not clearly seen in GOES SUVI imagery, but moving field lines can be seen from this region starting around 2026-03-03T08:35Z in STEREO A EUV 195.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.

cmes.note: Faint CME first seen to the NNW in SOHO LASCO C2 starting around 2026-03-03T15:00Z. It is also seen to the NNW in GOES CCOR-1 and very faintly in SOHO LASCO C3. This CME is covered by a real-time data gap in STEREO A COR2. The front is seen for a couple frames in SOHO LASCO C3, but the CME has dissipated enough by the time it reaches the FOV of SOHO LASCO C3 that it is not trackable for measurement. There is not separate source signature for this CME, and it is most likely outflow following CME: 2026-03-03T11:01Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement based on running difference imagery from SOHO LASCO C2 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the NNW in SOHO LASCO C2 starting around 2026-03-03T18:36Z part way into the FOV of SOHO LASCO C2. This feature is also seen to the NNW in SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1. It is covered by a real-time data gap in STEREO A COR2. There is not a separate source signature for this CME, and it is most likely outflow following CME: 2026-03-03T11:01Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement based on running difference imagery from SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the NW in SOHO LASCO C2 starting at 2026-03-04T00:48Z. This CME is also seen to the NW in SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1. It is covered by a real-time data gap in STEREO A COR2. The source of this CME is possibly related to some faint field line movement seen beyond the NW in SDO AIA 193 beginning around 2026-03-04T00:00Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Updated measurement using later frames of SOHO LASCO C3 imagery. A plane-of-sky measurement was made using a longitude of 90 degrees due to the far-sided and therefore unknown source location.
Preliminary measurement based on early frames from SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the east/southeast in SOHO LASCO C2, GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2A imagery. This event is not seen in SOHO LASCO C3 imagery as it is obscured by the pylon. The source is likely related to a filament eruption centered near S20E70 starting around 2026-03-04T01:00Z as seen in SDO AIA 131/171/193/304 and GOES SUVI 131/171/195/284/304 imagery. A subsequent opening of field lines is visible over the southeast limb in SDO AIA 171/193 and GOES SUVI 171/195/284 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the north in STEREO A COR2 imagery and to the north/northwest in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. The source may be related to minor brightening observed near N55W04 starting around 2026-03-04T07:30Z as seen in SDO AIA 193 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: Very narrow jet-like CME to the NW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1. There is no clear source for this CME.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 imagery. CME may be less than 10 degrees half-width.

cmes.note: CME visible to the west in SOHO LASCO C2. The CME is also faintly visible in SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2. The CME is not seen in GOES CCOR-1 due to Earthshine. The source of the CME is likely far-sided and not clearly visible in EUV imagery. STEREO A EUV imagery has a data gap from 2026-03-04T16:35Z to 2026-03-05T03:55Z. The potential source may be seen as a filament that gradually lifts off beyond the east limb in GOES SUVI 304 imagery starting around 2026-03-05T01:32Z. Moving field lines are also visible in SDO AIA 171 in ISWA.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.

cmes.note: Faint CME visible to the southeast in SOHO LASCO C2 imagery and possibly faintly to the southwest in STEREO A COR2 imagery. The CME is partially obscured by the pylon in SOHO LASCO C3. The CME is not visible in GOES CCOR-1 imagery due to Earthshine. No likely candidate source eruption is seen in available EUV imagery, and the CME is likely far-sided.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement focusing on the southernmost portion of the CME as seen in SOHO LASCO C2 and its best fit with STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.

cmes.note: Faint CME observed to the E in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, and GOES CCOR-1. The source is likely far-sided field line movement on or just beyond the equatorial east limb, observed in GOES SUVI 284 starting around 2026-03-05T22:00Z. The source longitude is not directly observed, but is likely E90-E100 based on the visibility of the field line movement.

cmeAnalyses.note: Single spacecraft analysis of the CME, taken originally as a plane-of-sky at E90, but considered a good approximation of the CME location to simulate. The source longitude could be E90-E100.

cmes.note: Faint CME observed to the NW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, not observed in GOES CCOR-1 or STEREO A COR2. No distinct source for this CME is observed in EUV imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: SOHO LASCO C2 only plane of sky, as the CME front becomes too faint to resolve in LASCO C3 running difference.

cmes.note: CME observed to the SE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. A GOES CCOR-1 eclipse briefly obscures early frames of the CME, and the LASCO C3 pylon obscures analysis in running difference imagery. The source is a small filament eruption centered around S25E30 starting around 2026-03-06T02:32Z in GOES SUVI 304, also observed as some brightening and field line movement in GOES SUVI 171/195/284/304 and some dimming in GOES SUVI 171/195.

cmeAnalyses.note: Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1. LASCO C3 pylon obscures the front in running difference imagery. The CME front is dimpled and somewhat diffuse, so this analysis encompasses as much of the bright material as possible.

cmes.note: Faint CME observed to the NW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1, starting difficult to resolve in LASCO C2 but becoming better defined further into the coronagraphs' field of view. May be faintly visible to the NW in STEREO A COR2, but too faint to resolve well, particularly in running difference imagery. The source is not clearly observed anywhere on disk.

cmeAnalyses.note: SOHO LASCO C3 only plane-of-sky, as the CME is too faint to resolve in LASCO C2.

cmes.note: CME observed to the E in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1, diffusing quickly. The source is likely field line movement observed over the equatorial east limb in GOES SUVI 284, starting around 2026-03-06T12:48Z, with a source likely E90-E100.

cmeAnalyses.note: Single spacecraft analysis of the CME, taken originally as a plane-of-sky at E90, but considered a good approximation of the CME location to simulate. The source longitude could be E90-E100. CME front slightly less than 10 degrees half-width.

cmes.note: Faint CME first seen to the SE by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-03-06T18:12Z. No clear source has been found in EUV imagery for this event, however there is a slow rising and opening of field line loops beyond the E limb, beginning after approximately 2026-02-06T15:48Z, as faintly seen in GOES SUVI 195 and 284, possibly indicating a far-sided source behind the limb.

cmeAnalyses.note: This far-sided CME is covered by a data gap in STEREO A COR2 coronagraph and no clear source is seen in EUV imagery. Only a plane-of-sky analysis with SOHO LASCO and GOES CCOR-1 imagery is possible. The CME has likely sped up with time, so this analysis is made with later C3 and CCOR-1 imagery, to capture the speed at 21.5 Rs.

cmes.note: A clear partial-halo front seen in STEREO A COR2 to the SW, with the start of the event covered by the nighttime data gap in STEREO A COR2 coronagraph. The start of this event is likely much earlier. This front in STEREO A likely (but not definitively) corresponds to one of several follow-up fronts to the 2026-03-06T18:12Z CME, possibly to the front that is first seen in SOHO LASCO C2 at 2026-03-07T00:00Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Very speculative, approximate analysis with two coronagraphs, initially based on the fitting of the clear halo front in STEREO A COR2A and later assuming that one of the less clear follow up fronts in SOHO/CCOR1 is the corresponding front (likely the front first seen in SOHO LASCO C2 at 2026-03-07T00Z), however this front is more of a speculation. The speeds in analysis can vary depending on the chosen half width, which in this analysis was based on the SOHO LASCO imagery and set to 37 degrees.

cmes.note: A faint and narrow CME front seen to the SE in COR2A. The source is likely far-sided, possibly indicated by the minor movement of field lines seen in GOES SUVI 195 on/behind the SE limb after 2026-03-07T09:30Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: This minor CME is only seen in STEREO A COR2 and not seen in other coronagraphs. The source is likely far-sided, therefore only plane-of-sky analysis is possible for this event.

cmes.note: A faint, narrow CME first seen to the SW in STEREO A COR2 starting around 2026-03-07T13:38Z. It is not seen in GOES CCOR-1 or SOHO LASCO C2/C3. A slow filament eruption on the SW limb in GOES SUVI 304 starting after 2026-03-07T09:30Z (and seen at the same time in STA EUV 304 close to the SW limb) was considered as a source candidate; however, there is too much time between this eruption and the start of the CME for this eruption to be the source. The source is most likely far-sided.

cmeAnalyses.note: Speculative plane-of-sky measurement based on running difference imagery from STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: A loop-like front to the ENE that is originally seen in SOHO LASCO as the southern part of a messy strange front conglomerate (likely two combined fronts). It is not seen in STEREO A because of a nighttime data gap. It is seen the clearest in GOES CCOR-1. The source of this CME is not clear.

cmeAnalyses.note: The CME is covered by the nighttime data gap in STEREO A and no clear source has been found for it, so it is likely far-sided. Only plane-of-sky analysis with GOES CCOR-1 is possible for this event.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2 starting around 2026-03-07T14:48Z. It is also seen to the NE in GOES CCOR-1. It is very faintly seen in SOHO LASCO C3 and is covered by a real-time data gap in STEREO A COR2. The front is the more northern portion of a two-part front seen in the NE. The more southern part is evaluated as CME: 2026-03-07T14:30Z. There is no good source candidate on the Earth-facing disk, so the source is most likely far-sided.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement based on running difference imagery from SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2 starting around 2026-03-7T15:36Z. It is only seen in SOHO LASCO C2 and dissipates before it reaches the end of the FOV of SOHO LASCO C2. It is not seen in GOES CCOR-1 or SOHO LASCO C3 and is covered by a real-time data gap in STEREO A COR2. There is no independent source signature for the feature. It is most likely outflow following CME: 2026-03-07T14:30Z and CME: 2026-03-07T14:48Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Single coronagraph plane-of-sky measurement using running difference imagery from SOHO LASCO C2.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the ENE in SOHO LASCO C2 starting around 2026-03-07T17:24Z. It is also seen to the ENE in SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1. The front is very slow to start, so analysis is based on the propagation of the CME once it reaches the later frames in the FOV of SOHO LASCO C2. There is no good source candidate on the Earth-facing disk, so the source is most likely far-sided. There is some faint, minor, nearly continuous field line movement seen beyond the E limb in SDO AIA 171 seen for several hours before and after the start of the CME that could be related.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement based on running difference imagery from SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the SW in SOHO LASCO C3 starting sometime before 2026-03-07T18:54Z. This feature first becomes visible part way into the FOV of SOHO LASCO C3. It is not seen in SOHO LASCO C2. It is covered by a real-time data gap in STEREO A COR2 and is obscured by the pylon in GOES CCOR-1. There is not a good source candidate on the Earth-facing disk, so the source is most likely far-sided. This CME follows a very similar trajectory to earlier CME: 2026-03-07T13:38Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Speculative plane-of-sky measurement based on limited frames from SOHO LASCO C3.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the E in SOHO LASCO C2 starting at 2026-03-07T23:00Z. It is also seen to the E in GOES CCOR-1 and SOHO LASCO C3. It is covered by a data gap in STEREO A COR2. There is no source candidate on the Earth-facing disk, so the source is most likely far-sided.

cmeAnalyses.note: Updated POS measurement using additional SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 frames.
Preliminary plane-of-sky measurement based on GOES CCOR-1 and early frames from SOHO LASCO C2.

cmes.note: Faint CME visible to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and N/NE in STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source appears to be a narrow moving/opening field lines visible along the NE limb in SDO/AIA 193 and 171 starting around 2026-03-08T09:29Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the Northeast by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-03-08T14:48Z as well as by STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. The source of this event is a small area of dimming and field line opening seen beginning at approx. 2026-03-08T13:39Z in SDO AIA 193. This eruption is located at approx. N15E40, but appears to exhibit some Northwestern deflection.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the East by STEREO A COR2 beginning at 2026-03-08T18:08Z, as well as by GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This CME is not visible in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 due to a data gap which began at 2026-03-08T16:12Z. The source of this event is a C2.0 flare and minor eruption seen in SDO AIA 131 and 304 peaking at 2026-03-08T15:07Z. This eruption was followed by a small dimming signature seen located at approx. N05E60 beginning at approx. 2026-03-08T17:04Z as seen in GOES SUVI 195.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in STEREO A COR2/GOES CCOR-1.
